changes_summary: Revised plan to address three significant flags: (1) Changed shot matching from ANY/partial overlap to FULL match using `every()` instead of `some()`, matching user's explicit requirement. (2) Fixed gallery ID source: uses `gallerySelectionMap.values().generationId` instead of `selectedGalleryIds` (which are item IDs, not generation IDs). (3) Added removal of `clearGallerySelection()` from `GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx:114` alongside the timeline fix — same bug, same fix. Also updated success criteria and assumptions to match the corrected semantics, added createdShot deduplication filter per user answer to Q3, and added gallery test run to validation step.

success_criteria.criterion: Right-clicking a clip shows existing shots that contain ALL of the selected generation IDs, with Jump to Shot and Generate Video buttons for each
Shots with only partial overlap of selected generation IDs do NOT appear in the existing shots list
Create Shot and Generate Video buttons still appear below existing shots
After creating a shot via context menu, the inline Jump to / Generate Video buttons show without duplication in the existing shots section
After closing and reopening the menu, the newly created shot appears in the existing shots section
Works in both ClipAction context menu (timeline) and SelectionContextMenu (gallery)
clearSelection() no longer runs after shot creation in timeline; clearGallerySelection() no longer runs after shot creation in gallery
Gallery existing shots lookup uses gallerySelectionMap.values().generationId, NOT selectedGalleryIds
All existing video editor, SelectionContextMenu, and gallery tests pass
No unnecessary re-renders — existing shots lookup uses useMemo with proper dependencies
Menu items for existing shots are visually distinct from creation actions (separator between sections)
Each modified file stays under ~300 lines
Full manual smoke test of all 7 scenarios in Step 7

assumptions: The clearSelection()/clearGallerySelection() calls after shot creation should be removed entirely in both timeline and gallery paths — the user can dismiss selections by clicking elsewhere.
No new database queries are needed — the client-side shots from useShots() already include images with generation_id, so filtering is cheap.
The gallery already has resolveSelectedGenerationIds() that extracts generation IDs from gallerySelectionMap values — the existing shots lookup follows the same pattern.

The createdShot local state deduplication (filtering out createdShot.id from existing shots) prevents the just-created shot from appearing twice while the inline Jump to / Generate Video buttons are visible.
